tasks.text: Create a Planning Hub page for this offsite
Coordinate hotel booking with travel agent
Send hotel confirmation numbers to attendees
Find nearest WeWork to hotel (walking distance)
Compile estimated costs across all categories
Draft budget approval request to Evelyn
Check Granola notes for recent leadership topics
Coordinate agenda topics with Evelyn & leadership
Confirm topic owners and time slots
Collect pre-read materials from topic owners
Send pre-reads to team (1 week before)
Send hotel confirmation details to attendees
Follow up on missing travel details
Confirm all bookings (hotel, venue, activity, restaurant)
Prepare DoorDash links for lunch orders
Send DoorDash link at start of first session
Follow up on action items and owners

lessonsLearned.lesson: Prioritize central/downtown location walkable to all activities. Avoid airport-area hotels.
Group activity + dinner is a proven combo. Escape room was a hit. Find unique activities.
Send DoorDash links at the start of sessions so food arrives on time.
Build flexible scheduling -- not every minute needs to be programmed. The team valued unstructured bonding time.
Agenda content and topics come from Evelyn and leadership -- Sophie coordinates and organizes, but doesn't set the agenda.
People did work beforehand - debating is useful. Pre-reads help drive better discussions.
Should've done more team building -- non-business activities matter.
Pacing matters. Starting late and going late works for some but not all (Azi disliked late dinner + early start). Schedule carefully.
Goal/rock setting improved dramatically when leaders came prepared with own models/POV.
More leading indicators vs. lagging indicators in discussions.
Require a single compiled pre-read document from all presenters.
Include recap/celebration of wins from prior quarter -- don't just focus forward.
Laptops down during sessions. People should not be missing meetings.
Clearer structure with specific talking points per session -- not just topic headings.
More push + pull in revenue makeup discussions and changes.
Dinner was too late -- schedule social dinner earlier.
Increased social pressure on getting pre-reads completed works.
Agree 2 weeks before on what each person wants to bring to the offsite.
Include deal review (close won vs. close lost) for learning.
Walking distance between all locations (hotel, venue, dinner, activity) is a huge advantage.

2024.name: 2024 Q1 Leadership Offsite
2024.summary: Mission & strategy, TAM analysis + ICP refinement, product roadmap, $4M investment discussion, pre-mortem on 2024 risks.
keyOutcomes: ICP defined: $5-50M GMV primary, Beauty/Health/Apparel verticals, Shopify + SFCC
TAM for segment: $5bn in marketing tech spend (3000 stores)
$4M investment discussion - more tangible than expected
Final mission & strategy to be written by Rishabh

ratingDetails.name: Aless
ratingDetails.feedback: $4M went better than expected, much more tangible

ratingDetails.name: Josh
ratingDetails.feedback: Wants more work

ratingDetails.name: Azi
ratingDetails.feedback: Got what you wanted out of it

ratingDetails.name: Shreyas
ratingDetails.feedback: Would be valuable to get one non-business activity

ratingDetails.name: Rishabh
ratingDetails.feedback: Debating is useful. Should've done more team building

ratingDetails.name: Abe
ratingDetails.feedback: Lots of meat, zooming in on what we want

ratingDetails.name: Rabah
ratingDetails.feedback: Structure strong, social event would've been nice

lessonsLearned: Pre-reads and debate are useful - people did work beforehand
Should've done more team building / non-business activities
Attach more data to discussion points

2024.name: 2024 Q3 Leadership Offsite
2024.summary: Urban hike, H2 reforecast, product roadmap, customer journey, ICP, pricing, pre-mortem. North star: 180 happy customers by Jan 1.
keyOutcomes: Company north star: 180 Happy Customers by Jan 1
Customer stages in SFDC to track evangelists
New pricing experiments with 3 new customers
lessonsLearned: Urban hike was a good team-building activity (low-intensity, inclusive)
Pre-mortem format identifies real risks: product, sales, marketing, competitors, team

2024.name: 2024 Q4 Leadership Offsite
2024.summary: Q4 financial plan, GTM moat/touchless, enterprise, agency, mid-market scaling, pricing, rock writing, partnerships. Pickleball/padel tournament.
keyOutcomes: Goal setting significantly better - clarity on who owns what
More leading indicators vs. lagging indicators
Build financial model beforehand for discussions

ratingDetails.name: Rishabh
ratingDetails.feedback: Everything was better - people came with own POV

ratingDetails.name: Shreyas
ratingDetails.feedback: Pacing was better. Rock setting went much better

ratingDetails.name: Hult
ratingDetails.feedback: Good reorientation on new initiatives

ratingDetails.name: Rabah
ratingDetails.feedback: Liked starting late and going late

ratingDetails.name: Evelyn
ratingDetails.feedback: Did not enjoy Mon/Tue orientation

ratingDetails.name: Azi
ratingDetails.feedback: Dinner + early start on Tuesday did not work out well

lessonsLearned: Starting late + going late works for some but not all
Rock setting improved dramatically when leaders came prepared with own models
More leading indicators vs. lagging indicators in discussions
Build financial model beforehand
Dinner + early start the next day was problematic

2025.name: 2025 Q1 Leadership Offsite
2025.venue: WeWork (600 B St, San Diego, CA 92101)
2025.summary: FY25 financial plan, product strategy (big bets), people/culture/comp, pre-mortem, rocks. Kayak tour + dinner at Malibu Farms. Pre-reads: The Effective Executive, High Output Management, Multipliers.
lessonsLearned: Book club / pre-read requirement drives better conversations
Kayak tour was a unique team building activity
Different city (San Diego) was refreshing

2025.name: 2025 Q2 Leadership Offsite
2025.summary: FY25 Q2 financial plan, tariffs + economy impact, product experimentation (international, consumer behavioral data), org structure decision, people management. Cooking class. Shirley's management presentation, hiring, pricing.

ratingDetails.name: Rishabh
ratingDetails.feedback: Not enough debate

lessonsLearned: More push + pull in revenue makeup discussions
Cooking class was a good social activity
Need more debate - discussions sometimes too harmonious

2025.name: 2025 Q3 Leadership Offsite
2025.venue: Soma Central (450 Townsend St.) + FERMAT Office
2025.summary: FY25 Q3 financial plan, GTM reprioritization + pricing (service pricing), EPD reprioritization, AI readiness. Dinner at Rich Table. Pre-read: Only the Paranoid Survive.

ratingDetails.name: Rishabh
ratingDetails.feedback: Dinner was too late

ratingDetails.name: Shreyas
ratingDetails.feedback: Tremendous execution risk

ratingDetails.name: Evelyn
ratingDetails.feedback: Debate

ratingDetails.name: Hult
ratingDetails.feedback: Identified future path with clarity, everyone bought in

ratingDetails.name: Saunder
ratingDetails.feedback: Really candid discussion

lessonsLearned: Dinner was too late - schedule social dinner earlier
Candid discussion was highly valued
AI readiness is an important recurring topic

2025.name: 2025 Q4 Leadership Offsite
2025.summary: Product roadmap, enterprise strategy, agency discussion, mid-market (MM), catch-all session, retro/rocks. Breakfast working session. First offsite for Jess, Isabel, Jennifer.
keyOutcomes: Clarity of execution is dramatically stronger
New team members (Jess, Isabel, Jennifer) integrated into offsites

ratingDetails.name: Shreyas
ratingDetails.feedback: Time management was medium. No wins recap. Mid-market could've been pushed more

ratingDetails.name: Evelyn
ratingDetails.feedback: Laptops down

ratingDetails.name: Hult
ratingDetails.feedback: Before and after helpful. Pre-read should be one compiled doc. Prioritize what should not be prio'd

ratingDetails.name: Bharat
ratingDetails.feedback: Need clearer structure, know major talking points. Outcomes were ok

ratingDetails.name: Saunder
ratingDetails.feedback: Most clear ever. Rishabh stating his mind better. A little messy

ratingDetails.name: Isabel
ratingDetails.feedback: Recap/celebratory things would help. Don't miss meetings

ratingDetails.name: Jess
ratingDetails.feedback: Preparation and pre-read needed. First time seeing info. People shouldn't miss meetings

ratingDetails.name: Jennifer
ratingDetails.feedback: One structure everyone follows. Broader company goals context needed

lessonsLearned: Require a single compiled pre-read document from all presenters
Include recap/celebration of wins from prior quarter
Laptops down during sessions
People should not be missing meetings
Clearer structure with specific talking points per session
One structure that everyone follows

2026.name: 2026 Q1 Leadership Offsite
2026.summary: The List, Rules of Engagement, Roles & Responsibilities, POC Commercials, Culture & Context, Retro. Escape Room at 4 Dreams Escape Game + Dinner at Earls Kitchen + Bar. Sophie's first offsite.
keyOutcomes: Escape room was a huge hit

ratingDetails.name: Rishabh
ratingDetails.feedback: Enough trust in room. All appropriately anxious. Being execution-biased is better. Could walk everywhere

ratingDetails.name: Shreyas
ratingDetails.feedback: Openness best we've seen, everyone else needs stronger opinions

ratingDetails.name: Evelyn
ratingDetails.feedback: Got what I needed, but less debate than expected

ratingDetails.name: Hult
ratingDetails.feedback: Aligned strategically. Agree 2 weeks before on what to bring

ratingDetails.name: Bharat
ratingDetails.feedback: Want 3-month summary + focus areas. More tactical. Pre-read social pressure

ratingDetails.name: Saunder
ratingDetails.feedback: Most optimistic and urgent. More aligned on strategy

ratingDetails.name: Isabel
ratingDetails.feedback: Enjoyed culture piece. Liked more tactical. Deal review (close won vs lost)

ratingDetails.name: Jess
ratingDetails.feedback: More collaboration. Could do more pre-reads. Resolved future state items

ratingDetails.name: Jennifer
ratingDetails.feedback: Helpful to get into tactics. Enjoyed culture. More focused on future state

lessonsLearned: Walking distance between all locations is a huge advantage
Escape room was a hit - find unique team activities
Increased social pressure on pre-reads works
Agree 2 weeks before on what each person wants to bring
Include deal review (close won vs. close lost)
Sophie attending was valuable for coordination
More tactical focus appreciated alongside strategic

hotelResearch.name: Courtyard by Marriott Midtown East
hotelResearch.pros: 5-min walk to WeWork 575 Fifth Ave & 430 Park Ave. Modern rooms, free Wi-Fi, 12th-floor lounge. Close to subway (4/5/6).
hotelResearch.bestPairedWith: WeWork 575 Fifth Ave or 430 Park Ave

hotelResearch.name: SpringHill Suites Midtown Manhattan
hotelResearch.pros: Large rooms by NYC standards, free breakfast included. Walk to WeWork 1460 Broadway. Near Grand Central.

hotelResearch.name: Renaissance New York Midtown
hotelResearch.pros: 348 rooms, floor-to-ceiling windows. Walk to WeWork Midtown West and Penn Station.

hotelResearch.name: Moxy NYC Chelsea
hotelResearch.pros: Fermat has automatic 10% off website rate. WeWork across the street. Alice (travel agent) negotiating further with GM Diana.
hotelResearch.aliceNote: Alice contacted Diana at Moxy for Fermat-specific discount. $1,120/room for both nights before discount. Awaiting final pricing.

hotel.name: Moxy NYC Chelsea (pending final pricing)
hotel.rateNote: ~$420/room with 10% Fermat discount, pending Diana confirmation via Alice

weworkResearch.name: 575 Fifth Avenue
weworkResearch.notes: Near Grand Central, Bryant Park. B/D/F/M/4/5/6/7 trains.

weworkResearch.name: 430 Park Avenue
weworkResearch.notes: Quieter location, close to Grand Central.

venue.name: WeWork (booked)
venue.costNote: Day pass conference rooms start ~$10/seat/hr. 10-person room for 2 full days.

dinnerResearch.name: The Modern
dinnerResearch.notes: Fine dining at MoMA. Impressive setting for a special occasion.

dinnerResearch.name: BLACKBARN
dinnerResearch.vegetarianOptions: Excellent -- seasonal vegetarian dishes built into menu
dinnerResearch.notes: Locally sourced, relaxed but upscale. Multiple private spaces.

dinnerResearch.name: Yara
dinnerResearch.notes: Great for diverse dietary needs. Fun shared-plates format like Denver dinner.

activityResearch.name: Escape Room (Escape Room Madness)
activityResearch.why: Proven hit from Denver. Great for problem-solving together.

activityResearch.name: Cooking Class / Great Guac Off
activityResearch.why: Relaxed, social, flexible participation. Fun competitive twist.

activityResearch.name: Improv Workshop (UCB Theatre)
activityResearch.why: Unique NYC experience. Builds communication & quick thinking.

activity.name: TBD
activity.lesson: Group activity + dinner is a proven combo. Escape room was a hit in Denver.

categories.notes: DoorDash group orders x2 days + dinner

themes.title: AI-First Transformation
themes.description: 10x contributor expectations, 3.5x engineering velocity, tool adoption training program. Rishabh shared strategy doc on 3/10.

themes.title: Outcome Ownership Framework
themes.description: Major gap: only Isabel completed the ownership exercise. Need 3-5 outcomes per leader defined.

themes.title: Hiring & Team Composition
themes.description: Bharat: 'how we hire is going to change a lot on eng.' Expect 30% reduction in 6 months. Culture favors high-curiosity.

themes.title: Enterprise Relationships & Account Management
themes.description: Tinuiti model discussion, AM role assignment, Jesse's role. Jess flagged need for clarity.

themes.title: Performance Reviews & Feedback Structure
themes.description: Saunder raised question about sharing packets with departing team members. Feedback structure discussion.

themes.title: Goal Alignment
themes.description: Shared vs. individual goals confusion. Multiple people assigned same goals (e.g., 'O'Keefe native').

themes.title: Speed & Execution
themes.description: Hard to set timelines during pivot, but speed = differentiator. 'Feeling of success' trap.

themes.title: AI for Customer-Facing Teams
themes.description: Jess: 'I get the how does Fermat incorporate AI question all the time.' Could be good for customer teams to have on hand.

coordinationContacts.name: Alice Zhao
coordinationContacts.reachOutFor: Hotel bookings, group rates, flight coordination

aliceCorrespondence.summary: Sophie initially asked about Courtyard Midtown East for 4 rooms. Alice suggested Moxy NYC Chelsea (Fermat has auto 10% off). Moxy quoted ~$1,120/room for both nights without discount. Alice contacted Diana (GM) for Fermat-specific pricing. Sophie confirmed Moxy is the likely choice pending budget approval. Alice is standing by for names, Marriott numbers, and which CC to use once approval comes through.
aliceCorrespondence.nextStep: Awaiting Moxy pricing from Diana via Alice. Need budget approval from Evelyn before committing.

keyEmails.summary: Initial request for Courtyard Midtown East, 4 rooms
Suggested Moxy Chelsea -- Fermat has 10% off. Moxy ~$469/room total ($1,120 for 2 nights before discount). WeWork across the street.
Confirmed likely going with Moxy, getting budget approval
Will wait on names/Marriott numbers and CC once approval comes
Asked Alice to check availability and rates at Moxy for Apr 20-21, 4 people
Contacted Diana at Moxy for Fermat-specific discount

lessonsLearned: Prioritize central/downtown location walkable to all activities. Avoid airport-area hotels.
Group activity + dinner is a proven combo. Escape room was a hit. Find unique activities.
Send DoorDash links at the start of sessions so food arrives on time.
Build flexible scheduling -- not every minute needs to be programmed. The team valued unstructured bonding time.
Agenda content and topics come from Evelyn and leadership -- Sophie coordinates and organizes, but doesn't set the agenda.
